titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul C. Daniels Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Christine Connolly and Carol King

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 8, 2018 at 11:21

did.abstract: The papers comprise correspondence; subject and research files; writings and speeches; and photographs documenting the personal life and professional career of Paul Daniels as a Foreign Service officer and diplomat.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials were the gift of Jean Daniels Portell, 2007.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul C. Daniels Papers (MS 1903). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Paul Clement Daniels was born in Buffalo, New York, on October 26, 1903. He graduated from Yale in 1924 with a degree in philosophy. After a brief time teaching, Daniels entered the Foreign Service in July 1927, serving as chairman of the Inter-American Coffee Board, 1941-1943; counselor of the Embassy, Bogota, Colombia, 1943-1945; Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, 1945-1947; ambassador to Honduras, 1947; director for American Republic Affairs, 1947-1949; and delegate to the Ninth International Conference of American States, Bogota, 1948; ambassador to the Council of the Organization of American States, 1948-1950; and ambassador to Ecuador, 1951-1953. He retired in 1953 but served as a special advisor on Antarctica for the United States Department of State, 1957-1959. Daniels died in 1986.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers comprise correspondence; subject and research files; writings and speeches; and photographs documenting the personal life and professional career of Paul Daniels as a Foreign Service officer and diplomat.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged chronologically by topic.

did.unittitle: "In the Clutches of the Scarlet Dragon"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Illustrated childhood story written with David Cavers.

did.unittitle: Yale photographs from the album of John Alden (Pete) Daniels, Class of 1919
Class of 1924 photographs, freshman and senior year

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and correspondence concerning commencement address.

did.unittitle: Oral history interview, Harry S. Truman Library, transcript
Letters of recommendation for French school and Foreign Service
Coffee Association scrapbook honoring Paul C. Daniels
Autographed photographs of Arthur Bliss Lane, Cornelia B. Lane, and Enrico Dutka
Photographs of Daniels and other dignitaries

unittitle.emph: Carta de la Organizacion de los Estados Americanos
Primera Aplicacion del Tratado Interamericano de Asistencia Reciproca

did.unittitle: Jamison, Edward A., Keeping Peace in the Caribbean Area
Official papers pertaining to conference and treaty
Daniels, Paul C., "The Antarctic Treaty," Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence concerning lectures and panel discussions.

did.unittitle: Pearson and Eaton, Inc. lecture management correspondence
